MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-14-25 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE OCT-14-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL, TO HAVE ALL OBJECTIONS DEEMED WAIVED, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA SEEKS AN ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF DYLAN HACKETT TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIFIED FORM INTERROGATORIES, AND SEEKS SANCTIONS AGAINST HACKETT AND HIS COUNSEL. THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS STATED HEREIN. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(301/CVA)

DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA'S MOTION TO COMPEL, TO HAVE ALL OBJECTIONS DEEMED WAIVED, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS [ADDED FOR POSTING OF PART 2 OF 2 OF THE TENTATIVE RULING].
Off Calendar Oct-14-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel, To Have All Objections Deemed Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. Chris Quattrociocche, Esq. of The Hackett Law Firm (tel: 415-410-9931) for Plaintiff.
Atty. Jeffrey Neustadt, Esq. of Law Offices of Jeffrey B. Neustadt (tel: 415-434-4440) for Defendant Alejandra Garcia.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA's Motion To Compel, To Have All Objections Deemed Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Alejandra Garcia seeks an order compelling plaintiff Dylan Hackett to provide further responses to specified form interrogatories, and seeks sanctions against Hackett and his counsel. The motion is granted in part and denied in part as stated herein. (See word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Continued To Oct-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Continued To Sep-10-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF DYLAN HACKETT's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Pursuant to the statement of nonopposition filed by defendant on July 2, 2025, the untimely motion to file a first amended complaint is granted. Plaintiff shall file within 10 days.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2025 Continued
